epic.name: On-Call Calendar UI
epic.objective: Build the frontend for viewing and managing on-call schedules, including a calendar view of upcoming shifts and override management.
epic.definition_of_done: Users can view who is currently on call, see upcoming shifts in a calendar, and create overrides — all from the web UI.

design_decisions.current_oncall_badge: Both: small badge in list view + prominent card at top of detail page
design_decisions.mobile_view: Simple vertical list: date + on-call user
design_decisions.color_assignment: On-the-fly HSL generation from stable hash of user_name (no storage)
design_decisions.override_created_by: Text input v0.4; auto-populate from auth in v0.9
design_decisions.participants_validation: Any string accepted (no validation); Slack user picker deferred to v0.6
design_decisions.active_schedule_definition: Any schedule with at least one layer

tasks.name: On-call schedules list and detail pages
tasks.description: Schedule list page and schedule detail page showing current on-call user and layer configuration
acceptance_criteria: GET /on-call → SchedulesPage: list all schedules with current on-call user badge (small colored badge)
GET /on-call/:id → ScheduleDetailPage: prominent current on-call card at top, name, timezone, layers list
Layer card: shows rotation type, participants in order, current shift user bold + highlighted + icon
Sidebar nav item: Calendar icon, label 'On-call', path /on-call (replaces disabled placeholder)
Empty state for no schedules with CTA to create first schedule (inline modal)
Loading skeleton and error states
tasks.implementation_notes: Follow existing page patterns (IncidentsListPage, RoutingRulesPage). Add useSchedules and useSchedule hooks. The 'current on-call' badge calls GET /api/v1/schedules/:id/oncall.

tasks.name: Shift calendar view
tasks.description: 2-week calendar grid embedded in ScheduleDetailPage showing on-call timeline
acceptance_criteria: Calendar renders a 2-week grid (week rows, day columns) by default
Each day cell shows the on-call user for that day derived from /oncall/timeline
Color-coded by participant: stable hash of user_name → HSL hue, generated on-the-fly
Navigate forward/back by week
Overrides visually distinct from base rotation (striped background or different shade)
Responsive: collapses to vertical date+user list on mobile (<768px)
tasks.implementation_notes: Build a simple custom calendar grid — no external calendar library. Use /oncall/timeline with ?from=&to= to fetch the rendered schedule. Color: djb2 hash of user_name → hue in HSL(hue, 65%, 55%).

tasks.name: Override management UI
tasks.description: UI for creating and deleting schedule overrides on ScheduleDetailPage
acceptance_criteria: Override button on ScheduleDetailPage opens OverrideModal
OverrideModal fields: override_user (text input), created_by (text input), start_time (datetime-local), end_time (datetime-local)
Validation: end_time > start_time, override_user required
Created override immediately reflected in calendar view (refetch timeline)
Overrides table below calendar, chronological (upcoming first)
Delete override button per row with window.confirm confirmation
Toast notifications for create/delete success/failure
tasks.implementation_notes: Follow inline modal pattern from RoutingRulesPage. created_by is text input in v0.4; will auto-populate from auth session in v0.9 (SSO). Toast: use existing Toast component.

tasks.name: Schedule create/edit UI
tasks.description: Forms for creating schedules and managing rotation layers
acceptance_criteria: Create Schedule button opens modal: name (required), description (optional), timezone (dropdown of top 30 IANA zones + UTC, alphabetical)

Add Layer panel on ScheduleDetailPage: name, rotation_type (daily/weekly/custom), rotation_start (datetime-local), shift_duration (number + unit for custom), participants (ordered list with add/remove, free-text)
Edit schedule metadata via Edit button: name, description, timezone
Delete schedule with window.confirm; warn if schedule has layers ('This schedule has active layers.')
New schedule navigates to detail page after creation
tasks.implementation_notes: Timezone list: top 30 by population + UTC. Participants: any free-text string; no validation. Slack user picker deferred to v0.6. shift_duration input: number field + select (hours/days) converted to seconds on submit.

deferred_items.reason: 2-week covers most use cases; toggle adds complexity for diminishing return in v0.4
Requires Slack user list API; free-text works fine for v0.4
No auth system exists yet; text input is sufficient placeholder
Low volume in v0.4; filter adds complexity
Order index can be set at creation; reordering is a nice-to-have
